author: @vossierebel
text: I used the vinigar/ salt solution to clean a few coins - it worked better than other soulutions I have used in the past! Thanks, another winner from Rob and Co!!👍😁

author: @donaldrussell-tx3873
text:
That rod you chose to clean was probably the easies shape to test on.
Would be much harder to clean small nuts and bolts by hand.

author: @docink6175
text: but the machines are so much more fun! I'll be doing a really bad off cast iron pot in a robert murray-smith vibrating cleaner later this week..

author: @undernetjack
text: Hi Rob, quick question: What is the suggested method (short and long term) of storing your Conductive Ink? I’ll need to know that for once I’ve won the Member’s giveaway this month, I’ll want to use some right away and save some for down the road 😎, thanks in advance. Cheers! Lol.

author: @hoverhead047
text: Instead of mineral oil, try fish oil for corrosion protection.

author: @chuxxsss
text: You are killing me Rob, lol. You should start a cleaning company.

author: @rodneyjack3309
text:
K.I.S.S. 
A little elbow grease? That's a missing component these days.

author: @ivanwalker3391
text: Nice one Robert. A truly "handy" little tip. Pssst! What's a Brillo pad? More power to yer elbow Bro! Ch-ears!

replies.author: @grahamepigney8565
replies.text: A Brillo pad is pretty much the same as an SOS pad, both invented at about the same time. A steel wire pad impregnated with soap.

replies.author: @ivanwalker3391
replies.text: @grahamepigney8565 I truly hope that my sarcasm is not wasted on you. ..... Really? Pssst! What's an SOS pad?

author: @prototype9000
text: Miracle cleaner is to get someone to do it for you

author: @thornwarbler
text: Aye the elbow grease is usually on the shelf next to the bags of grinder sparks  and above the shelf of tartan paint ;)

author: @littleworkshopofhorrors2395
text: Call the hand method "come the apocalypse cleaning".😁

author: @totherarf
text: Elbow Grease ..... to be applied by Apprentices' ;0)

author: @stevetobias4890
text: It's surprising at times just how fast you can clean by hand when using the right stuff. When you have a lot of stuff then for enough a vibrator or tumbler is exactly what is needed.

author: @colleenforrest7936
text:
It's also a matter of time too.
You can murky task with the machines :)

author: @williamarmstrong7199
text:
Looking at your subscriptions and views.. whats going on? Number of views is the same as when u had a fraction of your followers. 
Think u need to check on the subjects that are popular and go with them.

replies.author: @GigsTaggart
replies.text: I mean I subscribed for battery tech, obscure, cutting edge, advanced chemistry.   It's fine if he wants to do other stuff, but I just watched a video of a guy scrubbing a steering linkage and saying "hey scrubbing works".  This channel went from so complex I had to do further reading just to follow along, to so basic that it's almost kid stuff.  Variety is fine but I'm not sure this is worth attention anymore.

replies.author: @BigRich2580
replies.text: I think you're missing the point to this string of video's.

author: @ianelley
text: Forgive me if you have covered it but what about ultrasonic cleaning ? I know you can get small washing baths to do it but I've never seen anything bigger than a set up for jewelry

author: @livingladolcevita7318
text: in fairness a straight rod is easy to clean anyway. Parts with nooks and crannies are a lot more difficult

author: @tjacksonwoodworker3726
text: sometimes the simple solution is the best one.  Always take things by the smooth handle..

author: @Inventive101
text: Interesting, thank you

author: @Nuts-Bolts
text: How about now, tips for making your own tins of Elbow Grease?

author: @flytrapjohn
text: Lovely stuff.

author: @gerryheynes5082
text: Simplest can often be best - finished many jobs by hand that would have taken longer to get the equipment out to do! Well done Robert

replies.author: @gregorykusiak5424
replies.text: Simple is USUALLY the best, and this video is proof that with the right tools to make it simple, and a little bit of energy applying them, the job gets well done before you know it!

replies.author: @gerryheynes5082
replies.text: @gregorykusiak5424 ... and the chuckling from Robert after ‘3 minutes work’ is priceless!
